cards.back: A Nash Equilibrium where both players randomize their strategies, resulting in a probability distribution over all possible actions
The ability of players to randomize their actions in strategic games, which can lead to a more complex equilibrium
A Nash Equilibrium where both players choose a single action with probability 1, rather than randomizing over all possible actions
A strategy where a player randomizes over all possible actions, resulting in a probability distribution over all possible actions
A situation in game theory where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged
